channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida man to be executed for 1997 double murder witnessed by toddler
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of murdering a husband and wife during a fishing trip at a remote farm while the couple's toddler looked on is scheduled to receive a lethal injection Thursday evening in the state's first execution this year.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NATO is in disarray after the US announces that its security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In just one speech by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth this week, the most powerful member of NATO has thrown the world’s biggest military alliance into disarray, raising troubling questions about America’s commitment to European security.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hamas says it will free 3 more hostages as planned, paving the way to resolve ceasefire dispute
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas said Thursday it would release three more Israeli hostages as planned, paving the way toward resolving a major dispute over the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's pick for education chief seeks approval to lead an agency she would be asked to tear down
item.description: Linda McMahon faces an unusual test as she seeks Senate approval to lead an agency the president wants her to destroy.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Annie Ma, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas to execute man for 2004 murders of strip club manager and friend
item.description: Texas is scheduled to execute a man convicted of killing two men as part of a 2004 dispute with his boss at a strip club, and then years later prompted a massive lockdown of the state prison system when he used a cellphone smuggled into death row to threaten a lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vertuno And Michael Graczyk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge removes key legal hurdle for Trump’s plan to trim federal workforce with deferred resignations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday removed a key legal hurdle stalling President Donald Trump ’s plan to downsize the federal workforce with a deferred resignation program.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video shows the moment a passenger train smashes into an SUV, driver escapes with seconds to spare
item.description: A Utah Transit Authority train crashed into an SUV on Feb. 4, 2025, causing the vehicle to spin into a pole and completely destroy the front end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: BREAKING: Active shooter on Archer City Golf Course
item.description: Little information is known at this time, but we know it started around 4:30 p.m. at the Archer City Country Club golf course.
item.dc:creator: Angie Lankford, NewsNation

item.title: Former longtime Illinois legislative leader Michael Madigan is convicted in corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A Chicago Democrat who once set much of Illinois’ political agenda as the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history was convicted of conspiracy, bribery and wire fraud Wednesday in a split verdict in his high-profile corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs sues NBC over new documentary as he awaits trial on sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs is suing NBC Universal over a documentary that he says falsely accuses him of being a serial murderer who had sex with underage girls as he awaits trial on federal sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The relationship between the White House and its press corps is time-tested
item.description: This week, the White House barred Associated Press journalists from three media appearances by President Donald Trump — two of them in the Oval Office itself.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Legend says family is considering leaving California after wildfires
item.description: Last month's devastating wildfires may prompt John Legend and his wife, Chrissy Teigen, the latest celebrities to flee California.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. Navy fighter jet crashes near Shelter Island while attempting to land
item.description: A military plane crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island while attempting to land, according to the U.S. Navy.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Valentine’s Day scams to watch for this week
item.description: As Valentine's Day approaches, scammers are looking to deceive people. Here are the top scams to be aware of, and how to avoid falling for them.
item.dc:creator: Zach Myers, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to advance Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans on Wednesday released a blueprint they intend to use to advance President Trump’s legislative agenda, breaking through an impasse that had stymied the conference for days.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: AG Bondi holds press conference as concerns rise over DOGE
item.description: Her remarks come as President Trump has faced criticism over the decision to fire several DOJ prosecutors and FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Your closed Party City may soon turn into one of these familiar stores
item.description: What used to be your local Party City store may soon be selling books, recliner chairs, cowboy boots or discount merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Could obesity drugs help with alcohol cravings?: new study
item.description: Medications that have transformed the treatment of obesity may also help people drink less alcohol, according to new government-funded research.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two alleged serial shoplifter banned from every Walmart in the USA
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A serial shoplifter has gotten herself banned from every Walmart in the United States of America due to her history of stealing from the company, Memphis Police say. Ashley Cross, 37, is charged with criminal trespass and theft of merchandise less than $1,000.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: Egg prices have reached a record high in the U.S. The consumer price index released on Wednesday confirmed what consumers, bakers and food companies already knew: an ongoing bird flu outbreak that began in 2022 are making eggs terribly expensive right now.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As DOGE hammers away at the US government, Republicans stir with quiet objections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Katie Britt has been working to make sure the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency doesn't hit what she called “life-saving, groundbreaking research at high-achieving institutions,” including her state's beloved University of Alabama.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coca-Cola ICE raids? Company faces backlash in face of unfounded rumors
item.description: (KRON) -- A significant backlash has emerged against soft drink manufacturer Coca-Cola in the wake of rumors that the company laid off its own immigrant employees and then called the U.S. Immigration and Customs and Enforcement Agency on them. Rumors have circulated on X, Reddit, and Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs off on new steel and aluminum tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed proclamations to bolster tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the United States and crack down on attempts by China and Russia to evade penalties. Trump in 2018 announced 25 percent tariffs on steel and 10 percent tariffs on aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he has directed US Treasury to stop minting new pennies, citing rising cost
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin. “For far too long the United States has minted pennies which literally cost us more than 2 cents.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles family featured in Super Bowl commercial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Taco Bell marked its return to the Super Bowl with a commercial that featured nearly 400 fans, including a couple from Los Angeles and their newborn baby.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's is now limiting egg sales nationwide
item.description: Trader Joe’s patrons nationwide have taken to social media to comment on a new policy that limits the amount of eggs shoppers can buy. The company told Nexstar's KOIN that limits are currently in place at all of its U.S. locations.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Schumer warns of 'Trump shutdown,' lays out 4-pronged plan for Democrats
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) in a letter to colleagues Monday warned of the possibility of a “Trump shutdown” and reminded fellow senators that Democrats have the power to make or break any bill to fund the government past March 14.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, ABC 4

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people, while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, WGN 9
